page: Microsoft Azure E20ds_v4 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the EDSv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 20 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 10 physical Intel Xeon 8272CL cores with hyperthreading enabled. The processor operates at a base speed of 2.6 GHz and includes 35 MB of L3 cache. Memory capacity is 160.0 GB, yielding an 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio. Local storage is bundled with a 768 GB disk, and the server is Premium Storage capable. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Benchmark data places the E20ds_v4 consistently in the average performance tier across multiple workloads. The server achieves a single-core Geekbench score of 1271.0 and a multi-core score of 8298.0. In memory-intensive operations, it registers a read-only large block memory bandwidth of 80.0 GB/sec and a write large block bandwidth of 41.59 GB/sec. Database operations are supported by a Passmark database score of 7262.76 and a Redis SET throughput of 17.23 million operations per second. For language model tasks, the server processes 135M model prompts at 911.36 tokens per second, though performance drops to a weak tier of 30.52 tokens per second for prompt processing on a larger 7B model.

The E20ds_v4 offers high memory density and bundled local storage, making it a resource-dense option for memory-bound workloads. However, the underlying Intel Xeon 8272CL processor delivers moderate single-core performance, which may present a tradeoff for compute-heavy tasks that do not utilize the large memory footprint. The inclusion of 768 GB of local storage provides high-speed local scratch space without requiring external disk attachments, optimizing resource utilization.

This server configuration is suited for memory-intensive applications, relational database hosting, caching layers such as Redis, and software compilation. It is also appropriate for static web serving and moderate data processing tasks, but is less optimal for large-scale LLM inference or highly parallelized compute-bound workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure E20ds_v4 is a memory-optimized server from the EDSv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 20 dedicated vCPUs and 160.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance includes 768 GB of bundled local storage and is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark results position the server in the average tier, featuring a Passmark CPU score of 19266.4 and a Redis SET throughput of 17.23 million operations per second. Large block memory read bandwidth reaches 80.0 GB/sec. While the server delivers reliable performance for database operations and memory-intensive applications, its CPU-bound performance is moderate, as evidenced by a weak prompt processing speed of 30.52 tokens per second on 7B LLM models. The E20ds_v4 is best suited for relational databases, caching systems, and memory-heavy enterprise workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E20ds_v4 is a memory-optimized server featuring 20 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 160 GB of RAM, and 768 GB of local storage, designed for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E20ds_v4 is an Intel-powered memory-optimized server with 20 vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and 768 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring high memory density, bundled local storage, and balanced performance for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 20 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8272CL processors at 2.6 GHz.
160.0 GB of RAM providing an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio.
768 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Average-tier database performance with 17.23 million Redis SET operations per second.
Suited for relational databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive applications.
